excerpt: Complete breakdown of Goat Funded Trader pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Goat Funded Trader Pricing Overview

Goat Funded Trader stands out in the competitive prop trading space with one of the most aggressive entry points on the market: just $1/month to start trading. Founded in 2023 by Wishes Tower International Limited and based in Hong Kong, Goat Funded Trader offers six distinct challenge models ranging from ultra-affordable beginner accounts to premium instant-funding options. The platform supports forex, commodities, indices, metals, stocks, and over 500 crypto CFDs, making it a versatile choice for traders across multiple asset classes.

With a 4.1/5 rating and no free tier, Goat Funded Trader's low entry price ($1) serves as its primary value proposition for budget-conscious traders. However, the real value lies in the scaling potential: successful traders can reach Stage 4 with up to $2M in simulated capital and 100% profit splits, combined with on-demand payouts.

All Goat Funded Trader Plans Compared

Goat Funded Trader offers five primary pricing tiers, each designed for different trader profiles and capital availability:

Plan Name Monthly Cost Simulated Capital Best For
Goat $1 Challenge $1/mo Entry-level amounts (exact details vary) Complete beginners, testing the platform
Goat 2-Step $5K $26/mo $5,000 simulated capital Traders with basic experience seeking low-cost evaluation
Goat 2-Step $25K $119/mo $25,000 simulated capital Intermediate traders wanting substantial capital
Goat 2-Step $100K $499/mo $100,000 simulated capital Advanced traders targeting professional-level capital
Instant Goat $100K $899/mo $100,000 simulated capital with instant funding Traders wanting immediate access without multi-step evaluation

Key Differences Between Tiers:

  • Entry Price: The $1 Challenge is unbeatable for testing, but requires progression through evaluation stages to unlock profit splits and scaling
  • 2-Step vs. Instant: The 2-Step models ($26–$499/mo) require passing two trading phases before accessing profits. Instant Goat ($899/mo) skips this process but charges a significant premium
  • Capital Scaling: Traders who pass evaluations can reach Stage 4, unlocking access to $2M in simulated capital and 100% profit splits—a substantial advantage for profitable traders
  • Minimum Trading Days: Only 3 minimum trading days required on most evaluations (no indefinite time limits), allowing traders to complete challenges relatively quickly

The progression model rewards disciplined traders: starting at $1 and advancing through tiers can cost under $100 total if you succeed at each stage, making this one of the most accessible paths to funded trading.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Goat Funded Trader does not offer a free tier or free trial. However, the $1/month Goat Challenge effectively serves as the lowest-cost entry point to test the platform with minimal financial risk. For just $1, traders gain access to:

  • Simulated trading environment
  • Performance analytics dashboard
  • Risk management tools
  • Access to the mobile app (analytics only; trade execution requires desktop)

This makes the $1 tier worth starting with for absolute beginners. The financial commitment is trivial, and the evaluation requirements are achievable: traders need only 3 minimum trading days to complete the challenge. If you pass, you progress to the next tier. If you don't, your risk exposure is minimal.

Is it worth starting with? Yes, especially if you're new to prop trading or testing your strategy before committing larger funds. The ultra-low barrier ($1) removes objections to trying the platform, and the mobile analytics app lets you monitor positions on the go—though you'll need a desktop platform for actual trade execution.

Hidden Costs and Fees

While Goat Funded Trader's pricing appears transparent, several additional considerations affect total cost:

  • Failed Evaluation Refunds: The Instant Goat plan ($899/mo) carries higher upfront fees with no refund if you fail the evaluation. This differs from some competitors who refund a portion of fees upon completion, regardless of outcome
  • Trade Execution Platform Fees: Monthly subscription costs only cover your Goat Funded Trader account. Executing trades requires integrating with external desktop platforms (pricing for these platforms not included in Goat's fees, but may apply separately)
  • News Trading Restrictions: News trading profits are capped at 1% per trade. If your strategy relies on high-impact event trading, this limitation could reduce profitability and affect your ROI on monthly fees
  • Withdrawal/Payout Fees: On-demand payouts are available at Stage 4, but no specific withdrawal fees are detailed in the standard pricing. Confirm payout terms before scaling
  • Data Feeds: No mention of integrated real-time data feeds; traders may need separate subscriptions for premium market data

One positive: Goat accepts cryptocurrency payments, which may offer savings if you use crypto-friendly payment processors. The platform also runs frequent discounts up to 50%, so timing your purchase around promotional periods can reduce costs significantly.

Goat Funded Trader Pricing vs Competitors

How does Goat Funded Trader's pricing stack up against other popular prop firms? Here's a comparison with industry standards:

Prop Firm Lowest-Cost Tier $25K Capital Tier Instant Funding Option
Goat Funded Trader $1/mo $119/mo $899/mo
Funded Next (approximate) $75–$100 $150–$250 $500–$800
The Funded Trader (approximate) $99 $199–$249 $599–$799
Topstep (approximate) $150–$200 $299–$399 $999+

Note: Competitor pricing is approximate and based on typical industry rates. Actual prices vary by market conditions and promotional periods.

Goat Funded Trader's Competitive Position:

  • Unbeatable Entry Cost: At $1/mo, Goat's lowest tier undercuts nearly all competitors by $50–$200, making it the most accessible entry point for testing prop trading
  • Mid-Tier Value: At $119/mo for $25K capital, Goat is 20–50% cheaper than competitors at the same capital level
  • Instant Funding Premium: At $899/mo, Instant Goat is mid-range compared to competitors; some charge $500–$800, while established firms like Topstep charge $999+
  • Unique Advantage: Goat's 500+ crypto CFD support and multi-asset trading (forex, commodities, stocks) gives it an edge for diversified traders; most competitors focus narrowly on forex or futures

Verdict: Goat Funded Trader wins on affordability, especially for beginners and intermediate traders. It's strongest for traders who want to start cheap and scale progressively, while instant-funded competitors like Topstep appeal to traders willing to pay more for immediate capital access.

Is Goat Funded Trader Worth the Price?

For Different Trader Profiles:

  • Beginners ($1 Challenge): Absolutely worth it. The $1 entry is a no-risk way to test your strategy and familiarize yourself with the platform. The only risk is your time
  • Budget-Conscious Traders ($26–$119/mo): Excellent value. You get regulated, simulated trading capital with professional analytics and scaling potential. At $26–$119/mo, monthly costs are less than a single coffee subscription. If you're profitable, you'll earn back your fee many times over
  • Serious Traders ($499/mo 2-Step or $899/mo Instant): Worth the price if you're consistently profitable and value either the progression path (2-Step) or instant capital access (Instant Goat). However, the $899 instant option only makes sense if you can't wait 2–4 weeks for 2-Step evaluation completion
  • Professional/Scaling Traders: Very worth it. Reaching Stage 4 unlocks $2M capital and 100% profit splits—rare in the industry. On-demand payouts also mean you can withdraw profits regularly without annual restrictions

When to Upgrade:

  • Upgrade from $1 to $26 after passing your first challenge and proving your strategy works
  • Upgrade to $119–$499 tiers when you've consistently passed two 2-Step evaluations and want higher capital to scale profit growth
  • Upgrade to Instant Goat ($899) only if you need immediate trading capital and can't tolerate the 2–4 week 2-Step evaluation timeline

Value Score: 8.5/10 for beginners and intermediate traders; 7.5/10 for advanced traders (instant-funded competitors may offer better value if you prioritize speed over cost).

How to Save on Goat Funded Trader

Several strategies can reduce your total cost of trading with Goat Funded Trader:

  • Take Advantage of Frequent Discounts: Goat runs promotions up to 50% off regularly. If you're not in a rush, waiting for a promotional period can cut fees in half. Follow their social media and email list to catch deals
  • Use Crypto Payments: Paying with cryptocurrency avoids credit card processing fees and may qualify for additional discounts. This is especially useful if you already hold crypto
  • Start at $1, Progress Methodically: Rather than jumping to a $119 or $499 tier immediately, start at $1 and progress through tiers as you succeed. This costs less overall (cumulative: $1 + $26 + $119 + $499 = $645 to reach top tier) than if you paid $119 upfront
  • No Annual Lock-In Required: Unlike some competitors, Goat charges monthly with no annual commitment. You can pause or cancel anytime, so test during a promotional period and commit only if you're seeing results
  • Leverage Scaling Efficiently: Once profitable, reaching Stage 4 grants 100% profit splits and on-demand payouts. This means every trade profit goes directly to you—minimizing the impact of monthly fees

Bottom Line on Savings: The $1 entry tier is already the cheapest way to access prop trading. Beyond that, timing your purchases around the 50% discounts and using crypto payments are your best cost-reduction strategies. The real savings come from being profitable enough to reach Stage 4, where your 100% profit split makes monthly fees negligible.

For more details, check the Goat Funded Trader review page and explore our prop firm pricing comparisons to see how it stacks up against other funded trading platforms.
